task_text: Tests: planner output containing a fenced markdown block is written without the fences. Output without fences is written unchanged. Output with `~~~` fences is also handled.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013fAa8NuPJ3eyHzw6Sn2sNv","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /Users/mhcoen/proj/duplo/CLAUDE.md has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"117c0498-b09e-4ddd-a700-50dd1ced0c0a","uuid":"7c039142-d6c4-4f71-95de-5836caece77c","timestamp":"2026-04-16T04:42:57.524Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/Users/mhcoen/proj/duplo/CLAUDE.md","oldString":"test_planner.py: Tests for planner.py including `TestStripFences` covering `_strip_fences()` removing LLM code-fence wrapping: strips markdown fence, bare fence, md fence; no fence unchanged; inner fences preserved; leading/trailing whitespace handled; `generate_phase_plan` and `generate_next_phase_plan` strip fences.","newString":"test_planner.py: Tests for planner.py including `TestStripFences` covering `_strip_fences()` removing LLM code-fence wrapping: strips markdown fence, bare fence, md fence, tilde fence, bare tilde fence; no fence unchanged; inner fences preserved; leading/trailing whitespace handled; `generate_phase_plan` and `generate_next_phase_plan` strip fences.","originalFile":"# Duplo

Duplo duplicates apps. The user creates a project directory, drops
in reference material (screenshots, PDFs, text files, URLs), and
runs `duplo`. Duplo analyzes everything, identifies the product,
extracts features and visual design, and generates a plan. The user
then runs McLoop separately to build it. Running `duplo` again
detects new files and appends tasks for anything missing.

## Source files

### duplo/ (package)

- `main.py`: CLI entry point. Runs from the current directory with
  no required arguments. On entry, dispatches by subcommand:
  `fix` and `investigate` bypass the migration check and go
  straight to `_fix_mode()`; the default (no subcommand) path
  calls `_check_migration(Path.cwd())` from `migration.py`
  first — if the project is old-format, prints migration
  instructions and exits before any other work runs. After the
  migration gate passes, on first run (no `.duplo/duplo.json`):
  scans for reference materials via `scanner.py`, checks for a
  previously confirmed product identity in `.duplo/product.json`
  (via `load_product()` in `saver.py`) — if found, skips URL
  validation and product confirmation. Otherwise validates URLs
  point to a single product (via `validator.py`), fetches URLs,
  extracts frames from video files at scene change points (via
  `video_extractor.py`) — behavioral input is the union of
  ref/-declared behavioral-target videos and scraped demo videos
  from product-reference pages (`site_videos` from
  `_download_site_media`), processed together in a single pipeline
  call — filters frames with Claude Vision to keep
  only clear UI screenshots (via `frame_filter.py`), describes
  each frame's UI state (via `frame_describer.py`), stores accepted
  frames with descriptions in `.duplo/references/` (via
  `store_accepted_frames()` in `saver.py`), composes design
  extraction input via `collect_design_input()` from four sources
  (visual-target refs, visual-target video frames, site images,
  site video frames) with content-hash dedup — when no SPEC.md is
  present falls back to all images + all video frames — checks
  `spec.design.auto_generated` from the in-memory dataclass FIRST:
  if the AUTO-GENERATED block already has content, skips the Vision
  call entirely (write-once-never-replace semantics via
  `update_design_autogen` in `spec_writer.py`); otherwise sends
  the composed list to Claude Vision for design extraction
  (via `design_extractor.py`) and writes the result to SPEC.md's
  `## Design` AUTO-GENERATED block via `update_design_autogen`.
  `_source_url_from_spec()` returns the URL of the first
  non-proposed, non-discovered product-reference entry from a
  spec's sources list, or ``""`` if none.  Used by both
  `_first_run` and `_subsequent_run` to derive source_url from
  the spec instead of reading it from `product.json` or
  `duplo.json`.
  `_visual_target_video_frames()` helper matches accepted video
  frames back to their source videos via filename stems and
  filters to only those from videos with `visual-target` role
  in the spec (legacy helper; `_first_run` and `_analyze_new_files`
  now use the per-source lookup from `_run_video_frame_pipeline`
  via `_accepted_frames_by_source` instead of stem matching).
  extracts text from PDFs (via `pdf_extractor.py`), extracts
  text from docs-role references (via `docs_extractor.py`
  using `format_doc_references()` from `spec_reader.py`),
  extracts features, applies `scope_exclude` post-extraction
  filter via `_matches_excluded()` (from `extractor.py`) at the
  orchestrator level before saving, asks user to
  confirm and select, saves confirmed product identity to
  `.duplo/product.json` (via `save_product()` in `saver.py`) —
  `source_url` populated from the FIRST product-reference entry
  in `## Sources` for backward compatibility; new code reads from
  the in-memory spec via `_source_url_from_spec()`, not from
  `product.json`,
  generates roadmap, moves processed reference
  files to `.duplo/references/`, saves file hash manifest to
  `.duplo/file_hashes.json` (via `hasher.py`), creates Phase 1
  PLAN.md with design requirements, and runs McLoop. On subsequent
  runs: detects file changes via hash manifest, analyzes new/changed
  top-level files the same way as first run (images to Vision, PDFs
  to text extraction, URLs to scraper) via `_analyze_new_files()`
  (accepts optional `spec` parameter; when provided, uses
  `collect_design_input()` for role-based design input composition;
  otherwise falls back to all images + video frames),
  When SPEC.md declares scrapeable sources, uses
  `_scrape_declared_sources()` with `_persist_scrape_result()`,
  then downloads site media from product-reference pages via
  `_download_site_media()`, processes behavioral videos (ref/
  + site_videos) through `_run_video_frame_pipeline()`, composes
  design input from four sources with content-hash dedup via
  `collect_design_input()`, and runs design extraction (checking
  autogen first). Otherwise falls back to single-URL
  re-scrape via `_rescrape_product_url()`
  (accepts optional `spec` parameter for design input composition)
  to pick up site changes (updates reference URLs, raw pages, and
  code examples) — skips re-scrape if `last_scrape_timestamp` in
  duplo.json is less than 10 minutes old, re-extracts features from the updated scraped
  content via `extract_features()` and merges new ones into
  duplo.json via `save_features()` (without removing existing
  features), then compares the combined feature list, examples,
  and design requirements against PLAN.md via
  `_detect_and_append_gaps()`
  (appends new checklist tasks for uncovered items and design
  refinements — skipped when PLAN.md has unchecked tasks to avoid
  State 2 deadlock), prints a consolidated update summary via
  `_print_summary()`, then enters a three-state flow: (1) if
  PLAN.md exists and all tasks are checked, completes the phase
  via `_complete_phase()` (records history, advances
  `current_phase`, collects issues and feedback, captures
  screenshots) and falls through; (2) if PLAN.md exists with
  unchecked tasks, tells the user to run mcloop and returns;
  (3) if no PLAN.md, generates a plan for the current roadmap
  phase. If no roadmap exists or the existing roadmap is fully
  consumed (current_phase past last entry),
  `_partition_features()` splits features into implemented and
  remaining lists by `status` field, `_unimplemented_features()`
  returns just the remaining list, and `_print_feature_status()`
  displays the partition summary after phase completion.
  `generate_roadmap()` creates a new roadmap from the remaining
  features. `UpdateSummary`
  dataclass accumulates counts (files, images, videos, PDFs, URLs,
  pages re-scraped, gaps found, tasks appended) across the
  subsequent-run steps. `ScrapeResult` dataclass accumulates
  results from multi-source scraping (combined_text,
  all_code_examples, all_page_records, all_raw_pages,
  product_ref_raw_pages, merged_doc_structures, discovered_urls,
  source_records).
  `_scrape_declared_sources(spec)` iterates
  `scrapeable_sources(spec)` and calls `fetch_site` for each with
  its declared scrape depth, accumulating results with
  first-source-wins dedup by canonical URL. Collects cross-origin
  links from deep-crawl sources only for SPEC.md write-back.
  `_persist_scrape_result(result)` saves accumulated scrape
  artifacts to `.duplo/` (examples, page records, raw HTML, doc
  structures, per-source metadata via `save_sources`) and appends
  discovered URLs to SPEC.md via `append_sources` with
  `discovered: true`.
  Both `_first_run` (when SPEC.md has scrapeable sources) and
  `_subsequent_run` use these helpers instead of the single-URL
  fetch path. `_PROJECT_FILES` set excludes known
  project artifacts (PLAN.md, CLAUDE.md, etc.) from analysis.
  Subcommands: ``duplo fix`` records bugs and inserts
  fix tasks into the ``## Bugs`` section of PLAN.md via
  `append_to_bugs_section()` (from `saver.py`). Uses intelligent
  diagnosis: calls `investigator.investigate()` with the bug
  descriptions, user-supplied screenshots, and role-filtered
  context (counter-examples, counter-example source URLs, docs
  text, behavior contracts) via `_investigation_context()`,
  prints the structured diagnosis, converts diagnoses to fix
  tasks via `investigation_to_fix_tasks()`, and inserts them
  into ``## Bugs``.
  If investigator returns no diagnoses, falls back to simple text
  pipe mode, inserting undiagnosed fix tasks into ``## Bugs``.
  ``duplo investigate``
  (or ``duplo fix --investigate``) runs intelligent product-level
  diagnosis via ``investigator.py``, gathering all available
  product context (reference frames, frame descriptions, design
  requirements, current screenshot, features, code examples,
  open issues) and sending it to an LLM for structured diagnosis.
  Both accept ``--images`` for user-supplied screenshots and
  ``--screenshot`` to capture via appshot. ``--file`` reads bugs
  from a file. Without subcommands, state detection is automatic.
  `_compare_with_references()` compares current screenshot against
  reference images with backward-compatible fallback: first checks
  `.duplo/references/*.png` (canonical location for video frames
  and processed reference files), then falls back to
  `screenshots/*.png` for pre-migration projects. The fallback is
  intentional to maintain compatibility with older projects that
  store Playwright website captures in `screenshots/`. New projects
  use `.duplo/references/` exclusively. At the end of `main()`,
  calls `diagnostics.print_summary()` to print a one-line summary
  of any non-fatal failures logged during the run.

- `migration.py`: Migration detection for old-format duplo projects.
  `needs_migration()` returns True when `.duplo/duplo.json` exists
  but no valid new-format `SPEC.md` is present. New-format is
  detected by two independent signals (either sufficient):
  (1) marker string `"How the pieces fit together:"` anywhere in
  SPEC.md, (2) an `## Sources` heading via multiline regex. The
  regex intentionally matches `## Sources` even inside fenced code
  blocks (acceptable false-negative — a near-new-format file should
  not be force-migrated). Reads SPEC.md with `utf-8-sig` encoding
  to handle BOM-prefixed files. `_check_migration()` prints the
  migration message and calls `sys.exit(1)` when migration is
  needed; returns silently otherwise. `_MIGRATION_MESSAGE` constant
  contains the Phase 2 migration instructions (manual: author
  SPEC.md by hand using SPEC-template.md; `duplo init` does not
  exist yet).

- `scanner.py`: Scans the current directory for reference materials.
  `ScanResult` dataclass (images, videos, pdfs, text_files, urls,
  roles). `scan_files()` classifies a list of specific file paths
  into a ScanResult (used by subsequent runs for new/changed files).
  Accepts an optional `references` parameter (list of
  `ReferenceEntry`); when provided, each file's path is checked
  against `## References` to determine its declared roles, stored
  in `result.roles` (dict mapping Path to list of role strings).
  `scan_directory(ref_dir)` finds images (png, jpg, gif, webp),
  videos (mp4, mov, webm, avi), PDFs, text/markdown files, and
  extracts HTTP(S) URLs from any readable file (not just
  text-extension files). Callers pass `target_dir / "ref"` to
  scan the reference materials directory. Skips `.duplo/`, `.git/`,
  and other non-project directories. Skips binary/archive extensions
  (`_IGNORE_EXTS`). Deduplicates URLs while preserving order.
  No relevance scoring — all found files are included. Roles are
  declared in `## References`, not inferred from file properties.
  `check_unlisted_ref_files()` compares scanned files against
  `## References` entries and emits a non-fatal diagnostic via
  `record_failure("scanner", "io", ...)` for each file in `ref/`
  that has no corresponding entry. Diagnostic only — does not error.

- `fetcher.py`: HTTP fetching and HTML text extraction. `fetch_text()`
  fetches a single URL. `extract_text()` strips noise tags (script,
  style, nav, footer). `extract_links()` finds same-domain links.
  `score_link()` prioritizes docs/features over blog/pricing/legal.
  `is_docs_link()` detects documentation links by examining URL path
  and anchor text for docs-related words (not hardcoded platforms).
  `_PLATFORM_DOMAINS` set and `_is_platform_domain()` function
  prevent the crawler from following cross-domain links into hosting
  platform marketing pages (GitHub features, GitLab pricing, etc.)
  which would contaminate the feature list with platform features.
  Product documentation hosted on platforms (e.g.
  ``github.com/org/repo/wiki``) is allowed through — the function
  checks the URL path, not just the domain.
  `detect_docs_links()` extracts documentation links from HTML.
  `PageRecord` dataclass (url, fetched_at, content_hash) records
  each successfully fetched page.
  `fetch_site()` accepts a keyword-only `scrape_depth` parameter
  (`Literal["deep", "shallow", "none"]`, default `"deep"`).
  `"deep"` does BFS crawl following **same-origin** links only
  (same scheme + host + port); cross-origin links are not followed.
  `"shallow"` fetches only the entry URL with no link-following.
  `"none"` returns empty results without any network request.
  `_same_origin()` helper compares scheme, host, and port.
  `_is_html_content_type()` checks the Content-Type header; only
  `text/html` and `application/xhtml+xml` are accepted.
  Returns `(text, code_examples, doc_structures, page_records,
  raw_pages)` tuple where `raw_pages` is a `dict[str, str]` mapping
  each canonicalized URL (via `canonicalize_url`) to its raw HTML
  content. For every URL in `raw_pages` there is a corresponding
  `PageRecord`; failed fetches (404, timeout, non-HTML content-type,
  decode failure) appear in neither — both structures stay in sync
  by construction.
  Records fetch failures via `record_failure()` in `diagnostics.py`.

- `claude_cli.py`: Runs AI queries through the ``claude -p`` CLI
  instead of direct Anthropic API calls. `ClaudeCliError` exception
  for non-zero exit codes. `query()` sends a text prompt with
  optional system prompt and model selection. `query_with_images()`
  sends a prompt with image file paths, enabling the Read tool so
  Claude can view the images. All AI modules route through this
  helper so the Max subscription is used instead of API credits.

- `extractor.py`: Calls ``claude -p`` to extract a structured feature
  list from product content. `Feature` dataclass (name, description,
  category). `extract_features()` accepts `scraped_text` (the
  concatenation of text from all scrapeable sources — websites,
  documentation, PDFs, and other reference materials) plus optional
  `existing_names` parameter: when provided, the extraction prompt
  instructs the LLM to reuse existing feature names for matching
  concepts instead of inventing new ones (prevents near-duplicate
  accumulation across runs). The system prompt enforces strict
  anti-hallucination constraints: only demonstrably offered features,
  no platform features, no passing mentions, omit when in doubt.
  `_matches_excluded()` checks whether a feature matches any
  `scope_exclude` term using case-insensitive word-boundary regex.
  Compares against both `feature.name` and `feature.description`.
  Emits a diagnostic via `record_failure()` when a feature is dropped
  so false positives are visible in the run summary.
  Called at the orchestrator level (`main.py`) after
  `extract_features()` returns, not inside `extract_features()`
  itself.
  `_parse_features()` handles JSON parsing with fence
  stripping and error tolerance.

- `task_matcher.py`: Matches unannotated completed tasks to features
  using ``claude -p``. `match_unannotated_tasks()` filters tasks
  without ``[feat: ...]`` or ``[fix: ...]`` annotations, batches them
  into a single CLI call with the full feature list, and parses the
  response. For each task Claude classifies it as matching an existing
  feature ("existing"), representing new functionality ("new"), or
  being structural/scaffolding ("none"). Matched features are marked
  as ``implemented`` via `save_feature_status()`. New features are
  added to duplo.json with ``status: "implemented"`` and
  ``implemented_in`` set to the current phase via `save_features()`.
  `_parse_matches()` handles JSON parsing with fence stripping.
  Returns ``(matched_names, new_names)`` tuple.

- `gap_detector.py`: Compares extracted features, code examples, and
  design requirements against the current PLAN.md to identify gaps.
  Uses ``claude -p`` to determine which features/examples are not yet
  covered by the plan. `GapResult` dataclass (missing_features,
  missing_examples, design_refinements). `MissingFeature` dataclass
  (name, reason). `MissingExample` dataclass (index, summary, reason).
  `DesignRefinement` dataclass (category, detail, reason).
  `detect_gaps()` sends the plan, features, and examples via
  `claude_cli.query()` and returns a `GapResult`. Accepts optional
  `platform` and `language` keyword arguments so it can skip features
  that are infeasible for the target stack (e.g. Windows support for
  a macOS-only SwiftUI app). `detect_design_gaps()` compares design
  requirements (colors, fonts, components) against the plan text
  without an API call — returns a list of `DesignRefinement` for
  items not mentioned. `_parse_design_markdown()` parses the
  AUTO-GENERATED block markdown (from SPEC.md's `## Design` section)
  back into a design dict with the same shape as `duplo.json`'s
  `design_requirements`. `_merge_design_dicts()` unions two design
  dicts (first arg wins on key collision; components deduplicated by
  name). Both sources are merged at the orchestrator level
  (`_detect_and_append_gaps`) before calling `detect_design_gaps`
  (redundant during transition; can simplify in Phase 7).
  `_parse_result()` handles JSON parsing with
  fence stripping. `format_gap_tasks()` renders gaps (features,
  examples, and design refinements) as PLAN.md checklist items.
  Called during subsequent runs by `_detect_and_append_gaps()` in
  main.py after re-scraping and before phase execution.

- `design_extractor.py`: Sends reference images via ``claude -p``
  to extract visual design details. `DesignRequirements`
  dataclass (colors, fonts, spacing, layout, components,
  source_images). `extract_design()` passes up to 10 image paths
  to `claude_cli.query_with_images()` and parses the structured
  JSON response. `_parse_design()` handles JSON parsing with fence
  stripping. `format_design_section()` renders the extracted design as a
  Markdown section for inclusion in PLAN.md.
  `format_design_block()` produces the same content as
  `format_design_section()` but without the
  ``## Visual Design Requirements`` heading, for use inside the
  AUTO-GENERATED block in SPEC.md's ``## Design`` section. Called
  during first run on relevant images found by `scanner.py`.
  Results saved to duplo.json via `save_design_requirements()`.

- `orchestrator.py`: Orchestration helpers for duplo's multi-step
  pipeline. `_collect_cross_origin_links()` extracts cross-origin
  `<a href>` targets from fetched pages for SPEC.md discovered-link
  write-back. `collect_design_input()` builds the combined image
  list for design extraction from four sources: (1)
  `format_visual_references(spec)` paths (user-declared
  `visual-target` files in `ref/`, excluding `proposed: true`);
  (2) accepted frames from videos with `visual-target` in their
  roles; (3) images downloaded from product-reference sources via
  `_download_site_media`; (4) accepted frames from scraped
  product-reference videos. Deduplicates by resolved path and
  content hash. `_accepted_frames_by_source()` maps each video
  source path to its accepted (post-filter) frames as
  ``dict[Path, list[Path]]``. Input MUST be post-filter — callers
  run ``frame_filter.apply_filter`` before passing results. One-line
  dict comprehension but named for testability.

- `diagnostics.py`: Append-only diagnostics channel for non-fatal
  failures. `ERRORS_JSONL` constant (`.duplo/errors.jsonl`).
  `CATEGORIES` frozenset of valid categories: ``fetch``,
  ``screenshot``, ``llm``, ``hash``, ``io``.
  `record_failure()` appends a JSON-lines record with timestamp,
  site (``module:function``), category, message, and optional
  context dict. `count_failures()` returns the number of records
  in the log. `print_summary()` prints a one-line summary when
  failures exist (e.g. ``2 non-fatal failures logged to
  .duplo/errors.jsonl``). Called from `main()` at end of run.
  Failure sites that write records: `screenshotter.py` (page
  capture failures), `fetcher.py` (HTTP fetch and download
  failures), `investigator.py` (LLM query failures),
  `saver.py` (dedup LLM fallbacks), `video_extractor.py`
  (frame hash failures), `docs_extractor.py` (unknown file
  extensions).

- `pdf_extractor.py`: Extracts text content from PDF files.
  `extract_pdf_text()` accepts a list of paths, extracts text from
  all pages of each PDF, and returns the combined text with filename
  headers. `_extract_single()` handles one PDF file. Skips
  unreadable files silently.

- `docs_extractor.py`: Extracts text from docs-role reference files,
  routing by extension. `docs_text_extractor()` accepts a list of
  `ReferenceEntry` objects with `docs` role and produces combined
  text routed by extension: `.pdf` files go through
  `extract_pdf_text` (from `pdf_extractor`), `.txt` and `.md` files
  are read directly via `_read_text_file()`. Unknown extensions
  produce a diagnostic via `record_failure()` and are skipped.
  Each file's content is preceded by a
  `=== filename ===` header. Called during first run and subsequent
  runs on docs-role references found via `format_doc_references()`.
  Extracted text is included in the combined text sent to feature
  extraction.

- `video_extractor.py`: Extracts frames from video files at scene
  change points using ffmpeg. `ExtractionResult` dataclass (source,
  frames, error). `ffmpeg_available()` checks if ffmpeg is on PATH.
  `extract_scene_frames()` runs ffmpeg with the `select` filter
  using `gt(scene,threshold)` to detect visual transitions, saves
  frames as PNG files. Retries with a lower threshold if too few
  frames are found. After extraction, deduplicates similar frames
  using perceptual image hashing via `deduplicate_frames()`.
  `_dhash()` computes a 64-bit difference hash (dHash) for an image.
  `_hamming()` computes Hamming distance between two hashes.
  `deduplicate_frames()` compares each frame against kept frames
  and removes near-duplicates (Hamming distance <= threshold,
  default 6). Requires Pillow; gracefully skips deduplication if
  Pillow is unavailable. `extract_all_videos()` processes multiple
  videos. Called during first run and subsequent runs on relevant
  video files found by `scanner.py`. Extracted frames are fed
  into the frame filtering and design extraction pipelines alongside
  user-provided images. Frames are saved to `.duplo/video_frames/`.
  Records frame hash failures via `record_failure()` in `diagnostics.py`.

- `frame_filter.py`: Sends candidate video frames via ``claude -p``
  to classify each one. `FilterDecision` dataclass (path,
  keep, reason). `filter_frames()` sends frames in batches of 10
  via `claude_cli.query_with_images()`. `_filter_batch()` sends
  a batch and parses the response. `_parse_decisions()` handles JSON parsing
  with fence stripping; falls back to keeping all frames on parse
  error. `apply_filter()` returns kept frame paths and deletes
  rejected frames from disk. Keeps clear, stable UI screenshots;
  discards transitions, blur, marketing overlays, loading screens.
  Called by `main.py` after video frame extraction and deduplication,
  before frames are passed to design extraction.

- `frame_describer.py`: Sends accepted video frames via ``claude -p``
  to describe what UI state each one shows. `FrameDescription`
  dataclass (path, state, detail). `describe_frames()` sends frames
  in batches of 10 via `claude_cli.query_with_images()`.
  `_describe_batch()` sends a batch and parses the response. `_parse_descriptions()`
  handles JSON parsing with fence stripping; falls back to "unknown"
  state on parse error. Called by `main.py` after frame filtering,
  before design extraction. Prints each frame's UI state description.

- `hasher.py`: Computes and persists a SHA-256 hash manifest of all
  files in the project directory. `compute_hashes()` walks the
  directory tree (skipping `.duplo/`, `.git/`, etc.) and returns a
  `{relative_path: sha256}` dict. `save_hashes()` writes to
  `.duplo/file_hashes.json`. `load_hashes()` reads it back.
  `diff_hashes()` compares old and new manifests, returning a
  `HashDiff` dataclass (added, changed, removed). Called during
  first run (initial manifest) and subsequent runs (detect changes).

- `validator.py`: Validates that a URL points to a single clear
  product, not a company portfolio or homepage with multiple products,
  and not a landing page with unclear product boundaries.
  `ValidationResult` dataclass (single_product, product_name,
  products, reason, unclear_boundaries). `validate_product_url()`
  fetches the page and uses ``claude -p`` to classify it into one of
  three categories: single product, multiple products, or unclear
  boundaries (vague landing page where the product is not clearly
  identifiable). `_parse_result()` handles JSON parsing with fence
  stripping and fallback defaults. Called by `main.py` during first
  run before `fetch_site()`.

- `selector.py`: Interactive feature selection. Displays features
  grouped by category with numbers. Accepts "all", "none", ranges
  like "1-4,7", or comma-separated numbers. `select_features()`
  accepts optional `recommended` (list of feature names from the
  roadmap's next phase) and `phase_label` (e.g. ``"Phase 2: Title"``)
  parameters; when provided, those features are marked with `*` in
  the display, the recommendation line includes the phase name and
  numbered indices, and they are used as the default selection
  instead of "all". `select_issues()` displays open issues
  (filtering out resolved ones) with numbered selection using
  the same pattern; default is "none" (skip). `_recommended_indices()`
  returns 0-based indices of recommended features.
  `_parse_selection()` handles input parsing.

- `questioner.py`: Interactive build preferences. `BuildPreferences`
  dataclass (platform, language, constraints, preferences).
  `ask_preferences()` prompts for each with platform prefix matching.

- `build_prefs.py`: Parses `BuildPreferences` from `## Architecture`
  prose via an LLM call, replacing the interactive `ask_preferences()`
  flow. `parse_build_preferences(architecture_prose)` sends the prose
  to Claude with a structured-output prompt and returns a
  `BuildPreferences` with `platform`, `language`, `constraints`, and
  `preferences` fields. Returns all-defaults on empty input or LLM
  failure (with diagnostic). When the LLM returns no usable fields
  (all empty/default), returns `BuildPreferences()` with all defaults.
  `is_all_defaults(prefs)` checks whether all fields are at defaults.
  `validate_build_preferences(prefs)` returns a warning list when
  preferences are all-defaults (surfaced by callers in `main.py`
  using the same pattern as `validate_for_run` warnings).
  `architecture_hash(architecture_prose)` returns the SHA-256 hex
  digest for cache invalidation (stored as `architecture_hash` in
  duplo.json; re-parsed when `## Architecture` changes).
  `_parse_response()` handles JSON parsing with fence stripping and
  brace-extraction fallback. `_str_list()` coerces values to
  `list[str]`.

- `saver.py`: Writes `.duplo/duplo.json` with selections and
  preferences. `DUPLO_DIR` (`.duplo`), `DUPLO_JSON`
  (`.duplo/duplo.json`), and `PRODUCT_JSON` (`.duplo/product.json`)
  constants define the state directory layout.
  `_ensure_duplo_dir()` creates the `.duplo/` directory before
  writes. `save_product()` writes the confirmed product identity
  (name and source URL) to `.duplo/product.json`.
  `load_product()` reads it back, returning
  ``(product_name, source_url)`` or ``None`` if absent.
  `derive_app_name(spec, target_dir)` resolves the application name:
  (1) returns existing ``app_name`` from ``product.json`` if present
  (preserves user edits); (2) if spec has a ``product-reference``
  source, uses ``product_name`` from ``product.json``; (3) falls back
  to the project directory name. Saves the result to ``product.json``
  under ``app_name``.
  `save_features()` merges new features into duplo.json, using
  `_deduplicate_features_llm()` to detect semantic duplicates via
  a single batch ``claude -p`` call (e.g. "CLI tool" and
  "Command-line interface (CLI)" are recognized as the same
  feature). Falls back to exact-name matching if the LLM call
  fails. After merging, runs a post-merge pass via
  `_find_duplicate_groups()` over ALL feature names to detect
  near-duplicates that accumulated across runs (e.g. "Custom
  vocabulary / glossary" vs "Custom vocabulary").
  `_merge_duplicate_group()` keeps the most descriptive name
  (longest) and preserves ``status: implemented`` if any member
  has it. Prints "Merged N duplicate feature(s)." when merges
  occur. After dedup merging, `_propagate_implemented_status()`
  compares remaining pending features against implemented ones via
  a single LLM call; any pending feature semantically identical to
  an implemented one is marked as implemented (e.g. "Local offline
  transcription" pending when "Local on-device transcription" is
  implemented). Never removes existing features except via dedup
  merge. Called during subsequent runs after re-extraction.
  `save_feature_status()` updates a single feature's `status` and
  `implemented_in` fields by name. Raises `ValueError` if the
  feature name is not found or the status is invalid.
  `mark_implemented_features()` takes a list of `CompletedTask`
  objects and a phase label, collects unique feature names from
  their `[feat: ...]` annotations, and calls `save_feature_status()`
  for each. Silently skips features not found in duplo.json.
  Returns the list of feature names that were marked.
  `resolve_completed_fixes()` takes a list of `CompletedTask`
  objects, collects unique fix descriptions from their `[fix: ...]`
  annotations, and calls `resolve_issue()` for each. Silently
  skips issues not found in duplo.json. Returns the list of issue
  descriptions that were resolved.
  `save_issues()` replaces the top-level `issues` list in duplo.json.
  `add_issue()` appends a single issue (description, severity,
  timestamp) with duplicate detection by description. Validates
  severity is one of `critical`, `major`, `minor`.
  `save_issue()` appends an issue with `description`, `source`,
  `phase`, and `status` ("open") fields, plus an `added_at`
  timestamp. Skips duplicates by description.
  `resolve_issue()` finds an issue by description and sets its
  `status` to "resolved" with a `resolved_at` timestamp. Raises
  `ValueError` if no matching issue exists.
  `load_issues()` reads the issues list back.
  `clear_issues()` empties the issues list.
  `save_code_examples()` stores extracted code examples
  in duplo.json. `save_examples()` saves each code example as a
  separate JSON file in `.duplo/examples/<index>_<slug>.json` for
  review and editing. `load_examples()` reads examples back from
  `.duplo/examples/`, falling back to duplo.json for backward
  compatibility. `EXAMPLES_DIR` constant (`.duplo/examples`).
  `save_doc_structures()` stores extracted doc structures.
  `save_reference_urls()` stores `PageRecord` list (URL, timestamp,
  content hash) for all pages consulted during scraping.
  `save_sources()` merges per-source scraping metadata (url,
  last_scraped, content_hash, scrape_depth_used) into duplo.json
  under the `sources` key.  New entries are added, existing entries
  with matching URLs are updated, and entries for URLs no longer in
  the current scrape are preserved (idempotent state — removing a
  URL from ``## Sources`` leaves its metadata in duplo.json while
  the pipeline stops re-scraping it).
  `save_raw_content()` saves raw HTML for each scraped page to
  `.duplo/raw_pages/<content_hash>.html` so re-runs can diff against
  what changed on the product site. `RAW_PAGES_DIR` constant
  (`.duplo/raw_pages`).
  `save_design_requirements()` stores visual design requirements
  (colors, fonts, spacing, layout, components) extracted from
  reference images.
  `write_claude_md()` writes a CLAUDE.md template to target projects
  with appshot and debugging instructions. Non-destructive: if the
  file already exists, only appends sections whose headings are not
  already present.
  `save_frame_descriptions()` stores frame UI state descriptions
  (filename, state, detail) in duplo.json under `frame_descriptions`.
  `store_accepted_frames()` copies accepted video frames into
  `.duplo/references/` and saves their descriptions to duplo.json.
  `move_references()` moves processed reference files (images, PDFs,
  text files) into `.duplo/references/` to keep the project directory
  clean. `REFERENCES_DIR` constant (`.duplo/references`).
  `append_to_bugs_section()` inserts bug-fix task lines into the
  ``## Bugs`` section of PLAN.md with reopen-in-place semantics.
  If the section exists (anywhere in the file): existing entries are
  indexed by BOTH their fix-tag and body text (first occurrence wins
  on each key), so an incoming task can match by either key.
  Unchecked duplicates are skipped, checked ``- [x]`` entries
  matching an incoming task's key are unchecked in place (reopened
  at their original position), and truly new tasks are appended at
  the end of the section. Skips the file write entirely when content
  is unchanged (preserves mtime on idempotent runs).
  Returns the number of tasks that caused a write (insertions plus
  checkbox flips). Already-unchecked matches contribute 0.
  If absent, creates the section after the intro prose and before
  the first checklist item or second heading. Returns the number of
  tasks inserted or reopened. `_task_body()` helper extracts the
  text after the checkbox prefix for body-level comparison, matching
  both lowercase `[x]` and uppercase `[X]` checked boxes.
  `_task_key()` computes an identity key for a task line: returns
  the value of the ``[fix: "..."]`` annotation if present, otherwise
  falls back to `_task_body()` (the text after the checkbox prefix).
  Used by ``duplo fix`` and ``duplo investigate`` to route all bug-sourced
  tasks into the canonical location that mcloop prioritizes.
  Records LLM call failures via `record_failure()` in `diagnostics.py`.

- `initializer.py`: Creates target project directory with git init,
  a `.duplo/` subdirectory for Duplo's working state, and a
  `.gitignore` that excludes `.duplo/`.
  `project_name_from_url()` derives name from hostname.

- `roadmap.py`: Generates a phased build roadmap via ``claude -p``.
  `generate_roadmap()` produces a JSON array of phases (phase number,
  title, goal, features, test criteria). `format_roadmap()` renders
  it for terminal display. `_parse_roadmap()` handles JSON parsing
  with fence stripping.

- `planner.py`: Generates PLAN.md for a specific roadmap phase.
  `generate_phase_plan()` accepts a roadmap phase dict and produces
  a McLoop-compatible checklist scoped to that phase. Both
  `generate_phase_plan()` and `generate_next_phase_plan()` strip
  outer triple-backtick fences from the LLM response via
  `_strip_fences()` before returning, so PLAN.md is always clean
  Markdown even when the LLM wraps output in code fences.
  Heading format:
  `# <AppName> — Phase N: <Title>`. Accepts optional `phase_number`
  keyword to override the phase dict's number (derived from
  `phases` history length + 1 in main.py).
  `generate_next_phase_plan()` incorporates feedback and visual
  issues. `append_test_tasks()` appends doc-example test checklist
  items to a plan. `save_plan()` writes the file; on first write
  (file does not exist), injects an empty ``## Bugs`` section after
  the intro prose and before the first checklist item so mcloop can
  insert runtime-bug fix tasks into it. If PLAN.md already exists it
  appends new content after a blank line, preserving all existing
  checked and unchecked items. `_inject_bugs_section()` handles the
  insertion logic. `CompletedTask` dataclass
  (text, features, fixes, indent). `parse_completed_tasks()` parses
  checked ``- [x]`` lines from PLAN.md content, extracting the task
  description, ``[feat: "..."]`` feature annotations, ``[fix: "..."]``
  fix annotations, and indentation level. Used at phase completion
  to determine what was implemented.

- `screenshotter.py`: Uses playwright to capture full-page screenshots
  of product website URLs. `save_reference_screenshots()` launches
  headless Chromium, captures each URL. Records page capture failures
  via `record_failure()` in `diagnostics.py`.

- `doc_examples.py`: Extracts code examples from documentation HTML
  as input/expected_output pairs. `CodeExample` dataclass (input,
  expected_output, source_url, language). `extract_code_examples()`
  finds `<pre>`/`<code>` blocks and pairs them using three strategies:
  labeled pairs (input/output headings), Python doctest style (`>>>`
  prompts), and shell style (`$`/`%` prompts). Called automatically
  by `fetch_site()` on each crawled page; results stored in
  .duplo/duplo.json via `save_code_examples()`.

- `doc_tables.py`: Extracts feature tables, operation lists, unit
  lists, and function references from documentation HTML.
  `DocStructures` dataclass aggregates `FeatureTable`,
  `OperationList`, `UnitList`, and `FunctionRef` dataclasses.
  `extract_doc_structures()` scans `<table>`, `<ul>`/`<ol>`, `<dl>`,
  and `<code>` elements, classifying each by its nearest heading.
  Called automatically by `fetch_site()` on each crawled page;
  results stored in .duplo/duplo.json via `save_doc_structures()`.

- `test_generator.py`: Turns extracted documentation examples into
  unit test files. `detect_target_language()` checks the target
  project directory for build-system files (pyproject.toml → Python,
  Package.swift → Swift, Cargo.toml → Rust, go.mod → Go,
  package.json → JS/TS) and returns the language name or "unknown".
  `main.py` calls this before test generation; if the target is not
  Python (or unknown), `generate_test_source` and `save_test_file`
  are skipped with a message. `load_code_examples()` reads examples
  from .duplo/duplo.json. `generate_test_source()` produces a test
  file with one test function per example calling a `run_example()`
  stub, grouped into classes by source URL for easy failure diagnosis.
  `generate_parametrized_test_source()` produces a compact
  pytest-parametrized variant, also grouped by source URL.
  `_category_class_name()` derives a class name from a URL.
  `_group_by_source()` groups examples by source URL preserving
  original indices. `save_test_file()` writes the generated file.

- (To be created) Update module. `duplo update` re-scrapes the
  product, compares against existing features and PLAN.md, appends
  new unchecked tasks for anything missing. Never modifies existing
  tasks.

- `verification_extractor.py`: Extracts functional verification cases
  from video frame descriptions stored in duplo.json. The frame
  describer already captures expression/result pairs (e.g.
  ``"'Price: $7 × 4' with the result '$28'"``); this module uses
  ``claude -p`` to parse those into structured
  ``VerificationCase(input, expected, frame)`` objects.
  ``extract_verification_cases()`` sends frame descriptions to the
  LLM and returns parsed cases. ``format_verification_tasks()``
  renders them as PLAN.md checklist items (e.g.
  ``- [ ] Verify: type \`Price: $10\`, expect result \`$10\```).
  ``load_frame_descriptions()`` reads the ``frame_descriptions``
  list from duplo.json. ``_parse_cases()`` handles JSON parsing
  with fence stripping. Called during plan generation in both
  ``_first_run`` and ``_subsequent_run`` (State 3).

- `spec_reader.py`: Reads and parses ``SPEC.md`` from the project
  root. `ProductSpec` dataclass (raw, purpose, scope, scope_include,
  scope_exclude, behavior, behavior_contracts, architecture, design,
  references). `BehaviorContract` dataclass (input, expected).
  `read_spec()` reads the file and returns a `ProductSpec` or
  ``None`` if absent. `_parse_spec()` splits the Markdown into
  sections by heading, extracting known sections (Purpose, Scope,
  Behavior, Architecture, Design, References) into structured
  fields. Scope include/exclude lines are parsed with regex;
  behavior contracts are parsed from backtick-delimited
  ``\`input\` \u2192 \`expected\``` patterns. `format_spec_for_prompt()`
  wraps the raw text for LLM injection with an authority label.
  `format_scope_override_prompt()` formats scope overrides as an
  addendum to the feature extraction prompt.
  `format_contracts_as_verification()` renders behavior contracts
  as PLAN.md verification tasks. The spec is read by `main.py`
  in `_first_run()`, `_subsequent_run()`, and `_fix_mode()`,
  and threaded into `extract_features()`, `generate_roadmap()`,
  `generate_phase_plan()`, and `investigate()`.

- `spec_writer.py`: Text-layer helpers for modifying SPEC.md content.
  Independent of pipeline-stage modules (does not import from
  `extractor.py`, `design_extractor.py`, etc.). Imports only
  `spec_reader.SourceEntry` and `url_canon.canonicalize_url`.
  `append_sources(existing_spec_text, new_entries)` returns modified
  spec text with new `SourceEntry` objects appended to `## Sources`.
  Dedup-by-canonical: skips entries whose canonical URL already exists
  in the section (regardless of `proposed:` or `discovered:` flags).
  Idempotent: double-call returns the same result. Formats entries
  with their flags (`discovered: true`, `proposed: true`). If
  `## Sources` does not exist, creates it after `## Architecture`
  (if present) or at end of file.

- `appshot.py`: Python wrapper around McLoop's `bin/appshot` script.
  `capture_appshot()` runs the subprocess with app name and output path.
  Accepts a `timeout` keyword argument (default 60 seconds) passed to
  `subprocess.run()`. Returns -2 on timeout with a printed warning.

- `comparator.py`: Compares app screenshots against references using
  ``claude -p`` via `claude_cli.query_with_images()`. The comparison
  function `_compare_with_references()` in `main.py` checks
  ``.duplo/references/`` first (video frames from the product demo),
  falling back to ``screenshots/`` (Playwright website captures) for
  backward compatibility with pre-migration projects. The fallback is
  intentional and tested — new projects always use
  ``.duplo/references/`` so the fallback is only reached by older
  projects that predate the migration.
  `ComparisonResult` dataclass (similar, summary, details).
  `_parse_response()` parses structured output.

- `investigator.py`: Intelligent product-level bug diagnosis using
  LLM analysis. `Diagnosis` dataclass (symptom, expected, severity,
  area, evidence_sources, contradicts, avoids_pattern).
  `InvestigationResult` dataclass
  (diagnoses, summary, raw_response). `investigate()` accepts
  role-filtered context inputs: `counter_examples` (list of
  `ReferenceEntry` with counter-example role, included with "AVOID"
  label), `counter_example_sources` (list of `SourceEntry` with
  counter-example role, included as URL+notes with "AVOID" label —
  NOT fetched, declarative context only),
  `docs_text` (combined text from docs-role reference files),
  `behavior_contracts` (list of `BehaviorContract` ground-truth
  input/output pairs). Also gathers product context via
  `_gather_context()` (reference frames from
  `.duplo/references/*.png`, current screenshot from
  `screenshots/current/main.png`, frame descriptions, design
  requirements, features, code examples, and open issues from
  `duplo.json`), builds a structured prompt via `_build_prompt()`
  with image legend so the LLM knows which image is reference vs
  current vs user-supplied, and sends everything to Claude via
  `query_with_images()`. Falls back to text-only `query()` when
  no images are available. `_parse_result()` handles JSON parsing
  with fence stripping and brace-extraction fallback; parses
  `contradicts` and `avoids_pattern` fields from LLM response.
  `format_investigation()` renders diagnoses for terminal display
  with severity tags, including contradicts and avoids_pattern.
  `investigation_to_fix_tasks()` converts
  diagnoses into `- [ ] Fix: ... [fix: "..."]` lines for PLAN.md.
  Called by `_fix_mode()` in `main.py` when `--investigate` is set
  or when invoked as `duplo investigate`. `_investigation_context()`
  helper in `main.py` builds the role-filtered kwargs dict from
  a `ProductSpec` using `format_counter_examples()`,
  `format_counter_example_sources()`, `format_doc_references()`
  (via `docs_text_extractor()`), and `spec.behavior_contracts`.
  Records LLM query failures via `record_failure()` in
  `diagnostics.py`.

- `issuer.py`: Converts `ComparisonResult` list into `VisualIssue`
  list. `format_issue_list()` renders Markdown. `save_issue_list()`
  writes ISSUES.md.

- `collector.py`: Collects user feedback and known issues from file
  or interactive input. `collect_feedback()` and `_read_interactive()`
  accept `input_fn`/`print_fn` for dependency injection (testable
  without patching builtins). `collect_issues()` prompts the user for
  known issues with multi-line input support — each issue can span
  multiple lines, a blank line finishes the current issue, and an
  immediate blank line (or EOF) ends input entirely. Returns a list
  of issue description strings (empty list if none). Called by
  `_complete_phase()` after status tracking.

- `notifier.py`: Sends macOS notification and prints terminal banner
  when a phase completes.

- `runner.py`: Previously ran McLoop as a subprocess. Duplo no
  longer calls McLoop directly. The user runs McLoop separately
  after duplo generates the plan.

- `url_canon.py`: URL canonicalization for duplo. Imports stdlib only
  (`urllib.parse`). `canonicalize_url()` normalizes a URL to its
  canonical duplo form: (1) lowercase scheme and host; (2) strip
  default ports (80 on http, 443 on https); (3) strip fragment
  (#section); (4) strip trailing slash from ALL paths including
  root. Preserves query strings. Used by fetcher, spec reader,
  saver, and cross-origin link collection to ensure all URL
  comparisons use a single canonical form.

### Top-level files

- `PLAN.md`: Task checklist for building duplo itself.
- `README.md`: User-facing documentation.
- `CLAUDE.md`: This file. Read by Claude Code at session start.
- `mcloop.json`: Check config (ruff check, ruff format, pytest).
- `pyproject.toml`: Package config with httpx, beautifulsoup4, lxml,
  playwright, anthropic, Pillow dependencies.

### tests/

One test file per source module, mirroring the package structure:
test_claude_cli.py, test_main.py, test_fetcher.py, test_extractor.py, test_selector.py,
test_questioner.py, test_build_prefs.py, test_saver.py, test_initializer.py,
test_planner.py, test_screenshotter.py, test_appshot.py,
test_comparator.py, test_issuer.py, test_collector.py,
test_notifier.py, test_runner.py, test_doc_examples.py,
test_doc_tables.py, test_test_generator.py, test_scanner.py,
test_validator.py, test_design_extractor.py,
test_pdf_extractor.py, test_docs_extractor.py, test_hasher.py,
test_gap_detector.py,
test_frame_filter.py, test_frame_describer.py, test_video_extractor.py,
test_task_matcher.py, test_verification_extractor.py,
test_investigator.py, test_spec_reader.py, test_spec_writer.py,
test_roadmap.py,
test_migration.py: Tests for `needs_migration()` and
`_check_migration()` covering old layout, new-format with marker
string, new-format with `## Sources` heading, non-duplo directory,
corrupted duplo.json, zero-byte SPEC.md, BOM-prefixed SPEC.md,
`## Sources` inside fenced code block, marker inside HTML comment,
and message text snapshot pinning.
test_fetcher.py: `TestFetchSiteScrapeDepth` covers `scrape_depth`
parameter: `"none"` returns empty without fetching, `"shallow"` fetches
single page with no link-following, `"deep"` follows same-origin links
only, raw_pages keyed by canonical URL, records/raw_pages stay in sync.
`TestFetchSiteFailedFetches` covers failure exclusion: 404 (deep/shallow),
timeout (deep/shallow), non-HTML content-type (deep/shallow), XHTML accepted,
decode failure (deep/shallow), record_failure called on 404 and non-HTML,
mixed failures maintain records/raw_pages sync invariant.
test_main.py: Tests for main.py including migration gate integration
(old-layout exits, new-format passes, fix/investigate bypass), including `TestSubsequentRunSpecVerificationIndependent` covering SPEC verification tasks appended independently of video frames, `TestFixModeDiagnosis` covering duplo fix routing through investigator.investigate().
`TestFixModeSpecContext` tests that _fix_mode passes spec context to investigate(): counter-example references reach investigator kwargs; behavior contracts reach investigator kwargs; counter-example source URLs reach investigator kwargs; --investigate flag path also passes spec context.
`TestVisualTargetVideoFrames` tests `_visual_target_video_frames` helper: empty when no spec, empty when no frames, returns only frames from visual-target videos, excludes proposed visual-target, ignores non-video visual-target entries.
`TestAnalyzeNewFilesWithSpec` tests `_analyze_new_files` with spec: uses `collect_design_input` when spec provided, falls back to legacy without spec.
`TestScrapeDeclaredSources` tests `_scrape_declared_sources`: fetch_site called per source with correct scrape_depth; first-source-wins dedup for page_records and raw_pages; discovered URLs collected only from deep sources; product_ref_raw_pages only from product-reference role; doc_structures merged; empty sources returns empty result; fetch failure for one source continues to next; source_records populated per successfully scraped source with url, last_scraped, content_hash (SHA-256 of scraped text), and scrape_depth_used; failed sources do not produce source_records.
`TestPersistScrapeResult` tests `_persist_scrape_result`: saves examples, page records, raw content; discovered URLs trigger SPEC.md write-back via append_sources; SPEC.md not rewritten when unchanged; source_records persisted via save_sources; save_sources not called when source_records is empty.
`TestRunVideoFramePipelinePerSourceLookup` tests `_run_video_frame_pipeline` returns per-source accepted-frame lookup: maps each source video to its accepted frames; empty videos returns empty lookup.
`TestDesignInputPerSourceLookup` tests `_first_run` uses `accepted_by_source` lookup for design input composition: visual-target frames selected via per-source lookup (not stem matching); behavioral-only video frames excluded from visual-target set.
`TestAutogenBlockSkipsVision` tests autogen block check via in-memory dataclass: `_first_run` skips Vision when `spec.design.auto_generated` has content; `_first_run` writes autogen block to SPEC.md when absent; `_analyze_new_files` skips Vision when autogen present; `_rescrape_product_url` skips Vision when autogen present; no spec does not skip Vision; empty/whitespace autogen does not skip Vision.
`TestSubsequentRunSpecSourcesIntegration` tests `_subsequent_run` integration with spec-declared sources: downloads site media from product_ref_raw_pages; processes behavioral videos via _run_video_frame_pipeline; skips design extraction when autogen block present; non-spec path uses _rescrape_product_url instead.
`TestSpecSourceOfTruth` tests that the in-memory spec from `read_spec()` at the top of `_first_run`/`_subsequent_run` is the sole source of truth for ALL decisions: `read_spec` called exactly once per run; scope_exclude uses in-memory spec not disk re-read; scrapeable_sources receives the in-memory spec; `_persist_scrape_result` does not call `read_spec` (reads raw SPEC.md text only for write-staging).
`TestIntegrationUrlOnlySpec` tests URL-only SPEC.md (## Sources only, empty ref/) scrapes via `_scrape_declared_sources` without direct `fetch_site` calls; scan_directory returns empty for empty ref/.
`TestIntegrationRefOnlySpec` tests ref/-only SPEC.md (## References only, no ## Sources) produces plan without HTTP requests — neither `fetch_site` nor `_scrape_declared_sources` called.
`TestIntegrationBothSourcesAndRefs` tests both ## Sources and ## References contribute: scraped text and docs text both appear in feature extraction input.
`TestIntegrationProposedExcluded` tests `proposed: true` exclusion: proposed refs excluded from format_visual/behavioral/doc_references; proposed sources excluded from scrapeable_sources; proposed refs excluded from collect_design_input; subsequent run does not process proposed refs.
`TestIntegrationProposedRemoved` tests that after removing `proposed: true`, refs/sources appear in formatter results; accepted visual-target ref included in collect_design_input.
test_saver.py: Tests for saver.py including `TestWriteClaudeMd` covering references to `.duplo/references/` not `screenshots/reference/`, `TestAppendToBugsSection` covering ## Bugs section creation, appending, deduplication, and preservation of checked items, `TestAppendToBugsSection.test_reopen_checked_bug_in_place`, `TestAppendToBugsSection.test_reopen_preserves_position`, `TestAppendToBugsSection.test_reopen_and_append_mixed`, `TestAppendToBugsSection.test_reopen_idempotent`.
`TestAppendToBugsSection` includes tests for bugs section boundaries with H1 and H2 headings, and reopen-in-place semantics (unchecking `- [x]` entries in place when the same bug is re-reported).
`TestAppendPhaseToHistoryStageRegex` tests `append_phase_to_history` accepts Stage headings.
`TestTaskBody`: Tests for `_task_body()` helper including stripping unchecked/checked/uppercase checkbox prefixes, preserving annotations, and handling non-checkbox lines.
`TestTaskKey`: Tests for `_task_key()` identity key computation, including fix annotation priority, fallback to body, and handling of checked/unchecked lines.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionDedupByBody`: Regression tests ensuring dedup compares body not full line, preventing duplicate insertion when re-queueing fixed bugs.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionDedupByFixTag`: Tests dedup via `[fix: "..."]` annotation identity key, including reopening by fix tag with different wording, skipping unchecked by fix tag, fix tag priority over body, and body fallback without tags.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionDualIndex`: Tests dual-index behavior where existing entries are indexed by both fix-tag and body text (first occurrence wins on each key), including skip/reopen via body when entry has fix tag, skip/reopen via fix tag, and first-occurrence-wins for duplicate entries.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionIndentation`: Tests that reopening preserves original line indentation, including leading spaces, tab indents, flush-left lines, and deep indents with fix tags.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionIdempotentMtime`: Tests that file write is skipped when content is unchanged (mtime preserved), including skip-unchecked, reopen-does-write, append-new-does-write, and empty-tasks cases.
`TestAppendToBugsSectionWriteCount`: Tests return value counts only tasks that caused a write, including mixed new/reopen/skip cases, all skipped returns zero, all reopened returns count, and new section returns task count.
`TestDeriveAppName`: Tests for `derive_app_name()` including URL-based derivation using product_name from product.json, no-URL fallback to directory name, product.json written with app_name, user-edited product.json not overwritten on subsequent runs, None spec falls back to directory, non-product-reference sources ignored, product-reference with empty product_name falls back to directory.
`TestSaveSources`: Tests for `save_sources()` including file creation, correct path, sources stored with all fields, preserves existing duplo.json fields, empty sources, merges preserving removed URLs (entries for URLs no longer in the scrape list are kept), updates existing entries by URL (same URL gets new metadata without duplication), file ends with newline.
test_url_canon.py: Tests for `canonicalize_url()` covering each rule individually (lowercase scheme/host, strip default ports, strip fragment, strip trailing slash), combined rules, root-path slash stripped, non-root path slash stripped, already-canonical unchanged, query string preserved with root slash gone, non-default ports preserved.
`TestSourceUrlFromSpec`: Tests for `_source_url_from_spec()` helper including first product-reference URL returned, proposed/discovered entries skipped, non-product-reference skipped, None spec and empty sources return empty string, docs-preceding product-reference still found.
`TestProductJsonBackwardCompat`: Tests backward compatibility of product.json source_url field: subsequent run syncs product.json when spec URL differs from saved; subsequent run uses spec URL (not duplo.json) for roadmap generation; no-spec fallback reads source_url from duplo.json.
`TestRemovedSourceIdempotent`: Tests that removing a URL from ## Sources leaves its entry in duplo.json (idempotent state) while the pipeline stops re-scraping it: removed source not passed to fetch_site; cached text not in scrape result; save_sources merges preserving removed entry; source_records only contains actually-scraped sources.
test_planner.py: Tests for planner.py including `TestStripFences` covering `_strip_fences()` removing LLM code-fence wrapping: strips markdown fence, bare fence, md fence; no fence unchanged; inner fences preserved; leading/trailing whitespace handled; `generate_phase_plan` and `generate_next_phase_plan` strip fences.

## Keeping this file current

**If you add, rename, or significantly change any source file, update
the relevant entry in this CLAUDE.md file before finishing.** This file
is the manifest that every future session reads first. If it is stale,
sessions waste time searching for files instead of working.

## Conventions

- Python 3.11+, ruff for linting, pytest for tests
- Lines must not exceed 99 characters
- Do not import pytest unless you use it (e.g., pytest.raises, pytest.fixture)
- Do not use `l` as a variable name (ruff E741). Use `line`, `link`, `item`, etc.
- Run `ruff format .` as the absolute last thing before finishing. After every edit. No exceptions.
- Run `ruff check .` after formatting to catch unused imports
- Do not chain shell commands with && or ;
- `## Bugs` section in PLAN.md: mirrors mcloop's contract — absolute priority, contains only unchecked bug task items, created at top of PLAN.md (after intro/architecture note, before first phase heading) on first write if absent. Route bug-sourced tasks through `saver.append_to_bugs_section()`. Feature tasks append at EOF after the bugs section. Never modify, reorder, or delete existing bugs entries.

## Debugging

When something crashes or behaves unexpectedly, find and read the
actual error output first. Check crash reports, stderr, log files,
tracebacks. Do not guess from source code alone. After fixing,
reproduce the failure and verify it is gone.
